perturbation.original_text: Heritage will keep, and will ensure that Jasper keeps [***] complete and accurate records in connection with each unique production lot of Products with respect to manufacturing practices, quality assurance measures, analytical procedures and their resultant data.
perturbation.changed_text: Heritage will endeavor to keep records in connection with each production lot of Products with respect to manufacturing practices, quality assurance measures, analytical procedures and their resultant data.
perturbation.explanation: The phrases 'endeavor to keep' and removal of '[***] complete and accurate records' create ambiguity and weaken the obligation to maintain thorough records. This introduces uncertainty about the level of detail and accuracy required, and if a 'complete and accurate record' is not present, they will not be held liable for not doing so.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 21 CFR § 111.475 - What records must you keep?
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 111.475 requires detailed records of the production and control of dietary supplements, including batch production records, equipment cleaning and maintenance, and distribution. By using 'endeavor to keep' instead of 'will keep' and removing the requirement for complete and accurate records, the modified text contradicts the regulation's mandate for comprehensive documentation, potentially leading to non-compliance and difficulty in verifying adherence to quality standards. Heritage is only held to 'endeavor' to create these records, rather than be required to have them.

perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the original requirement to keep 'complete and accurate records' to 'endeavor to keep records,' which contradicts the mandate in 21 CFR § 111.475 for comprehensive documentation of dietary supplement production and control. The scraped snippet, 'Under this subpart M, what records must you make and keep?' indicates a requirement to maintain records, and the modification introduces ambiguity and reduces the obligation to ensure thorough documentation, potentially leading to non-compliance with regulations regarding dietary supplement production.

perturbation.original_text:
Heritage and Jasper shall maintain insurance of the following kinds and in the following amounts during the Term of this Agreement:
i. Commercial General Liability Insurance with a limit of $[***] each occurrence and $[***] in the aggregate, including Contractual, Completed-Operations and Product-Liability Coverage with a limit of $[***] for each occurrence, covering both bodily injury and property damage liability.
perturbation.changed_text:
Heritage and Jasper shall obtain insurance of the following types during the Term of this Agreement:
i. Commercial General Liability Insurance, including Contractual, Completed-Operations and Product-Liability Coverage, covering both bodily injury and property damage liability.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text removes the specific insurance amount limits for each occurrence and in the aggregate, making it unclear what level of coverage is required. Replacing 'maintain' with 'obtain' suggests a one-time action, rather than ongoing maintenance of coverage. This creates ambiguity regarding the adequacy and continuity of insurance coverage, failing to meet the original obligation.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Insurance Code § 304 requires that an insurance contract specify the amount of the insurance. The modified text omits the specific coverage amounts, leaving it undefined, which violates the statutory requirement for certainty in insurance agreements, and also removes a level of protection initially afforded to Premier.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, INS 304 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Insurance Code - INS DIVISION 1. GENERAL RULES GOVERNING INSURANCE [100 - 1879.8] ( Division 1 enacted by Stats. 1935, Ch. 145. ) PART 1. THE CONTRACT [100 - 679.75] ( Part 1 enacted by Stats. 1935, Ch. 145. ) CHAPTER 2. Parties, Events, and Interests [150 - 305] ( Chapter 2 enacted by Stats. 1935, Ch. 145. ) ARTICLE 5. Insurable Interest: Effect of Transfer [300 - 305] ( Article 5 enacted by Stats. 1935, Ch. 145. ) 304. In the case of partners, joint owners, or owners in common, who are jointly insured, a transfer of interest by one to another thereof does not avoid insurance, even though it has been agreed that the insurance shall cease upon an alienation of the subject insured. (Enacted by Stats. 1935, Ch. 145.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the specific insurance amount limits, which contradicts the Law Explanation's assertion that California Insurance Code § 304 requires an insurance contract to specify the amount of the insurance. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing California Insurance Code - INS, supports the existence of insurance code regulations, even if it doesn't directly state the specific requirement about specifying the amount. The removal of the insurance amount creates ambiguity and violates the principle of certainty in insurance agreements, therefore a legal contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Heritage agrees to disclose to Premier and provide a list, upon request, of any material violations or deficiencies noted during any inspection by the FDA, United States Department of Agriculture, PHS, or any other federal, state or local health or food regulatory agency of the Heritage Facilities, Jasper Facility or any other Facility used to produce the Products, which have a material adverse effect on the manufacture or packaging of the Products.
perturbation.changed_text: Heritage will make reasonable efforts to inform Premier of any violations or deficiencies noted during inspections by regulatory agencies that Heritage believes may impact the Products.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces ambiguity by replacing the direct obligation to disclose 'any material violations or deficiencies' with a weaker obligation to 'make reasonable efforts to inform' Premier of violations that Heritage 'believes may impact' the Products. This subjective interpretation and weakening of the duty to disclose information contradicts the original intention of ensuring Premier is fully informed of potential regulatory issues. A violation is only disclosed if Heritage deems the violation impacts the products. This also may allow Heritage to avoid disclosure of minor violations or deficiencies.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 21 U.S. Code § 335b - Authority to request and receive records
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 U.S. Code § 335b grants the FDA broad authority to request and receive records related to food safety. By limiting the disclosure to violations or deficiencies Heritage *believes* may impact the Products, and only making 'reasonable efforts' to disclose, the modified text could impede the FDA's (and by extension Premier's) access to complete information necessary for ensuring the safety and compliance of the products. The original text stated 'any material violations or deficiencies', which ensures complete transparency.

Civil penalties (a) In general Any person that the Secretary finds- (1) knowingly made or caused to be made, to any officer, employee, or agent of the Department of Health and Human Services, a false statement or misrepresentation of a material fact in connection with an abbreviated drug application, (2) bribed or attempted to bribe or paid or attempted to pay an illegal gratuity to any officer, e
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts the original contract's intent to ensure Premier is fully informed of potential regulatory issues, creating an Ambiguity Legal Contradiction. The change from a direct obligation to disclose 'any material violations or deficiencies' to 'make reasonable efforts to inform' of violations Heritage 'believes may impact' the Products, weakens the duty to disclose information. The original text ensures transparency, whereas the changed text gives Heritage the power to decide what gets disclosed. Scraped Law Snippet 1, 21 USC 335b, discusses civil penalties for false statements to the Department of Health and Human Services, suggesting the importance of accurate reporting. While the snippet doesn't explicitly mandate disclosure to Premier, the modified text's weakening of the disclosure requirement could impede access to complete information, which could eventually lead to inaccurate reporting issues that the law seeks to penalize.
